title: App takeover checklist | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)

seo.title: App takeover checklist | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)
seo.description: A practical checklist for taking over an existing application: access, environments, release flow, risks, and critical workflows.

page.title: App takeover checklist | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)
page.h1: App takeover checklist after a supplier or team change
page.description: A practical checklist for taking over an existing application: access, environments, release flow, risks, and critical workflows.

hero.title: Takeover without a checklist leaves too many blind spots
hero.subtitle: The checklist helps surface what is missing for safe continuation of delivery and operation.

intro: Application takeovers are rarely only about the code. They often break down because access, environment visibility, release knowledge, or operational context is incomplete.
A practical checklist reveals the blind spots early instead of letting them appear later as costly surprises.

sections.title: What the template should cover
body: The point is not paperwork for its own sake. The value is clearer decision-making and fewer project blind spots.

sections.title: How to use it in practice
body: The template should work as a practical aid before an introductory call, scoping session, or takeover phase.

sections.title: What outcome it should create
body: A good template shortens the path to the first useful project decision.

faq.question: Is the checklist enough without an audit?
faq.answer: Often not. It is a great starting point, but complex applications usually need technical review and prioritisation too.

faq.question: Is this useful for internal teams as well?
faq.answer: Yes. Internal teams also need structure when important knowledge has left with a specific person or vendor.

faq.question: What if some access is missing?
faq.answer: That is exactly the kind of issue the checklist should surface immediately.

notFor: collecting detail with no decision attached
